programme: Degree Programme in Computer Science and Engineering
title: Introduction to Programming
name: Introduction to Programming
learning_outcomes: Upon successful completion students are able to: - Write, compile and run simple programs - Understand basic control structures (loops, conditionals) - Apply fundamental data structures (arrays, lists)
content: This course covers the basics of programming using Python: variables, control flow, functions, basic data structures, and simple algorithms. Includes weekly exercises and a final project.

programme: Degree Programme in Computer Science and Engineering
title: Data Structures and Algorithms
name: Data Structures and Algorithms
learning_outcomes: Upon successful completion students are able to: - Analyze algorithm complexity using Big-O notation - Implement common data structures (trees, graphs, hash tables) - Select appropriate algorithms for a given problem
content: Covers trees, graphs, hash tables, sorting and searching algorithms, and algorithm analysis. Includes lab sessions and programming assignments.

programme: Degree Programme in Computer Science and Engineering
title: Machine Learning Basics
name: Machine Learning Basics
learning_outcomes: Upon successful completion students are able to: - Explain core supervised and unsupervised learning concepts - Train and evaluate basic ML models - Apply regularization techniques to avoid overfitting
content: Introduces supervised and unsupervised learning, regression, classification, clustering, and neural network fundamentals using Python and scikit-learn.
prerequisites: Data Structures and Algorithms, Linear Algebra

title: Protein Chemistry I
name: Protein Chemistry I
learning_outcomes: Upon successful completion students are able to: - Tell the relevance of proteins, their structures and function - Understand methods for purifying recombinant proteins - Analyze biochemical data related to protein/enzyme purification
content: This module provides detailed information on the chemistry of proteins, including protein folding, degradation, enzyme catalysis, and purification/characterization methods. Includes lectures and laboratory exercises.
prerequisites: Biomolecules for Biochemists, Biochemical methodologies I

programme: Courses for exchange students - Centre for Language and Communication Studies
title: Academic English Writing
name: Academic English Writing
learning_outcomes: Upon successful completion students are able to: - Structure academic texts clearly - Use appropriate academic register and citation practices - Give and receive peer feedback on written drafts
content: Focuses on academic writing conventions, structuring arguments, citation practices, and peer review. Suitable for exchange students at any level.
